rulings.Case Title: KAZUKO K. ARTUS VS. GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement Pursuant To C.C.P 664.6 Request For Attorney Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for March 13, 2025, line 1. DEFENDANT GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCATION'S Motion To Enforce Settlement Pursuant To C.C.P 664.6 Request For Attorney Fees And Costs. Defendant's Motion to Enforce Settlement is GRANTED. Plaintiff breached the settlement agreement. A judgment shall be entered in accordance with the terms of the Settlement Agreement. Defendant to prepare an order and a form of judgment and present them to Plaintiff for review as to form no later than 9:00 a.m. on March 13, 2025. Defendant to present an order and judgment to the Court for review execution at the hearing. Defendant's request for attorney's fees is granted. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LINDA L. PERRY VS. JAMES A. PERRY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Notice Of Motion And Motion For Interlocutory Judgment Of Partition, Appointment Of Appraiser And Broker Pursuant To The Partition Of Real Property Act; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Request For Judicial Notice; Declarations Of Scott Talkov, James Perry, Deborah Perry, Matthew Taylor
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for March 13, 2025, line 6. CROSS COMPLAINANTS JAMES PERRY AND DEBORAH PERRY, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEES' Motion For Interlocutory Judgment Of Partition, Appointment Of Appraiser And Broker Pursuant To The Partition Of Real Property Act. Cross-Complainant's Motion for Interlocutory Judgement of Partition. Hearing Required. No later than 9:00 a.m. on March 13, 2025 each party to (1) file a declaration stating whether parties agreed to the value of the property or to a method of valuation other than appraisal; (2) if parties have not agreed on the value of the property or method of valuation, other than appraisal, is party to state their suggested value and their suggested alternative method of valuation. Additionally, Plaintiff shall include in their declaration a name of a proposed appraiser. =(501/hlj) pARTIES MAY APPEAR IN-PERSON, TELEPHONICALLY OR VIA zOOM (vIDEO - wEBINAR id: 160 560 5023; pASSWORD: 172849; OR pHONE dIAL IN: (669) 254-5252; wEBINAR id: 160 560 5023; pASSWORD: 172849). pARTIES WHO INTEND TO APPEAR AT THE HEARING MUST GIVE NOTICE TO OPPOSING PARTIES AND THE COURT PROMPTLY, BUT NO LATER THAN 4:00 P.M.
